items.skill_name: (iii) Calculate the distance between station A and station B
items.student_prompt: An electric shuttle travels in two parts: 12 m/s for 40 s, then 8.0 m/s for 55 s. Calculate the total distance travelled.
items.worked_solution: Distance in first part = 12 × 40 = 480 m. Distance in second part = 8.0 × 55 = 440 m. Total distance = 480 + 440 = 920 m.
items.marking_guidance: 1 mark for each section distance; 1 mark for adding the two distances; answer must include m.
items.common_misconception: A common error is to average the speeds without considering the different times. Calculate each section separately.
items.teacher_note: Use this to introduce piecewise motion before speed-time graph area.
items.originality_statement: Original Quanta Aptus content generated from derived skill metadata only.
validation_warnings: calculation resource: marking_guidance does not mention 'unit' or 'units' — consider adding unit award criteria
